events.note: Legacy-auth sign-in success (IMAP4 / BAV2ROPC) — basic auth bypasses MFA and Conditional Access. #1 ATT&CK technique in Red Canary 2025 report.
Risky interactive sign-in from anonymized IP (Tor/VPN) that SUCCEEDED — Conditional Access gap

mutation_hints.objective: Authenticate with valid cloud credentials while avoiding MFA and risk-based Conditional Access controls
detection_anchors: Field match: ClientAppUsed=IMAP4 or POP3 or 'Other clients' → change to 'Browser' or 'Mobile Apps and Desktop clients' — legacy-auth specific rules won't fire; the sign-in is still suspicious due to location/risk but avoids the legacy-auth detection anchor

Field match: RiskLevelDuringSignIn=high → change to medium or low — the Identity Protection risk engine scores based on threat intel; low-risk sign-ins from anonymized IPs are possible if the IP isn't yet in the blocklist
Field match: RiskEventTypes=anonymizedIPAddress → change to unfamiliarFeatures or remove the field entirely — some rules specifically anchor on anonymizedIPAddress (Tor/VPN indicator)

Field match: AuthenticationRequirement=singleFactorAuthentication → change to multiFactorAuthentication — if the tenant allows SSPR or soft MFA bypass, the sign-in could register as MFA-satisfied while still being attacker-controlled
Field match: ConditionalAccessStatus=notApplied → change to success — appears the sign-in passed CA evaluation (some rules only alert when CA didn't apply AND risk is high)
Field: Location → change from high-risk countries (RU/CN/KP/NG) to a medium-risk or unusual-but-not-flagged location — country-based rules have known blind spots
Field: AppDisplayName → change from 'Microsoft Azure PowerShell' (attacker-typical) to 'Microsoft Office' or 'SharePoint' — app-specific rules won't fire
